expectations.text: Claude reads package.json and source files before asking any question
expectations.evidence: Response lists stack details, architecture, files found - clearly read the codebase

expectations.text: Claude presents a KNOWN list of 3-5 things it inferred from the codebase
expectations.evidence: Uses headers/table format instead of a concise KNOWN list - information is there but scattered across sections

expectations.text: Claude correctly identifies Next.js, Supabase, TypeScript, and Vitest
expectations.evidence: Stack section lists 'Next.js 14.2, React 18, Supabase, TypeScript, Zod, Vitest, ESLint'

expectations.text: First question is about the product/what the project does, not about technical setup
expectations.evidence: First question: 'What is this task manager for? Who uses it?'

expectations.text: Questions include concrete options (not open-ended) and a recommendation
expectations.evidence: Question is open-ended: 'Is this a personal productivity tool or a team product?' - no structured options A/B/C, no clear recommendation

expectations.text: Claude asks exactly one question per message, not multiple
expectations.evidence: Asks 3 questions in one message: what is it for, are there plans for features, is auth handled
